title: Add prediction mode to all 11 distributed systems simulations
description:
The single most impactful practice feature. Before each step/action, prompt the user to predict the outcome. ZERO prediction features exist currently.

Examples per simulation:
- Raft: 'Which node will win the election?'
- Consistent Hashing: 'How many keys will move?'
- CAP: 'Will this write succeed in CP mode?'
- Gossip: 'After this round, how many nodes will have the data?'
- CRDTs: 'After merge, what will A's value be?'
- 2PC: 'P2 voted NO. Commit or Abort?'

After the action, show whether prediction was correct with brief explanation.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 prediction prompts per simulation (22 minimum total)
Predictions appear BEFORE the action
Correct/incorrect feedback with brief WHY explanation
Prediction accuracy tracked per concept in progress system
User can disable predictions

title: Add manual tracing mode for top 5 concepts (user IS the algorithm)
description:
In manual tracing mode, the user performs the algorithm's operations by clicking/dragging, and the system validates.

1. Raft Election: User clicks nodes to send RequestVote, validates majority.
2. Consistent Hashing: User clicks the correct node on the ring for a given key.
3. CRDT Merge: User fills in the merged vector.
4. Gossip: User selects which peers to gossip to.
5. 2PC Coordinator: User receives votes and decides Commit/Abort.

Each exercise has 3 rounds of increasing complexity.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 manual tracing exercises (one per top concept)
Validation checks user's actions against protocol rules
Wrong actions show feedback
3 difficulty levels per exercise
Completion tracked in progress

title: Surface existing interview questions from concepts-data.ts in simulation properties panel
description: concepts-data.ts has 4-5 interview questions per concept with model answers — already written, just not shown in the module. Surface as 'Test Your Understanding' expandable section.
acceptanceCriteria: Interview questions visible for 6+ concepts
Questions expandable to reveal model answers
Styled consistently with properties panel

title: Fix module-progress.ts — only tracks 6 of 11 distributed concepts
description: module-progress.ts:82-89 tracks: cap-theorem, consistent-hashing, raft-consensus, gossip-protocol, vector-clocks, crdt. Missing: two-phase-commit, saga-pattern, map-reduce, lamport-timestamps, paxos.
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 distributed concepts in the progress array
IDs match SIMULATIONS array in DistributedModule.tsx

title: Add 5 comparison challenges: side-by-side protocol showdowns
description:
1. 'Raft vs Paxos: Same cluster, same failure. Which recovers faster?'
2. 'Lamport vs Vector Clocks: Same events. Can Lamport detect concurrency?'
3. '2PC vs Saga: Payment service crashes. Which blocks, which compensates?'
4. 'CP vs AP: 1000 writes during partition. How many succeed?'
5. 'Gossip fanout=1 vs fanout=3: How many rounds to convergence?'

Each: scenario → user predicts → run both → show result → explain WHY.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 comparison challenges implemented
User predicts the outcome before seeing results
Explanation of WHY one protocol performs differently
Score tracked

title: Add 5 scenario-based selection challenges (which protocol for this problem?)
description:
1. 'Chat app needing message ordering → Raft/Gossip/CRDTs?'
2. '10M cache keys across auto-scaling servers → technique?'
3. 'Three offline offices editing same doc → merge strategy?'
4. 'Payment must charge card AND reserve inventory → protocol?'
5. '100 servers detecting which are alive, no coordinator → technique?'
acceptanceCriteria: 5 scenario challenges implemented
Each has 3-4 protocol options
Feedback explains WHY correct AND WHY others don't fit
Score tracked

title: Add 3 debugging challenges: find the bug in this protocol
description:
1. Raft: 'Election timer not reset on valid heartbeat.' User identifies missing timer reset.
2. CAP: 'CP mode accepts writes from minority partition.' User identifies missing majority check.
3. CRDT: 'G-Counter merge uses SUM instead of MAX.' User identifies wrong merge op.

Each: show buggy behavior → user identifies bug → reveal fix with explanation.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 debugging challenges implemented
Each shows visually broken behavior
User must identify the specific bug
Explanation shows what invariant the fix protects

title: Connect existing gamification infrastructure to distributed module
description:
The platform has achievements, leaderboard, streak tracking, difficulty scaling — NONE connected to distributed module.

1. streak-protector.ts → daily practice streak
2. achievements.ts → 5 distributed achievements
3. difficulty-scaling.ts → adaptive difficulty for predictions
4. leaderboard.ts → score from practice activities
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 achievements specific to distributed module
Streak counter visible in module UI
Difficulty adapts based on prediction accuracy
Leaderboard entries from practice

title: Add distributed-specific weekly challenge and daily micro-challenge
description:
weekly-challenge.ts has only 1 distributed scenario. daily-challenge.ts has none.

Weekly: 'Design system surviving 2 failures using Raft', 'Achieve gossip convergence in min rounds', 'Build CP system with quorum reads'
Daily: 'Min quorum for 5-node cluster?', 'After 3 gossip rounds, how many nodes?', 'True/False: AP reads always return latest write?'
acceptanceCriteria: 3 weekly challenges
7 daily micro-challenges
Integrated into existing challenge framework

title: Add Socratic Tutor integration for distributed simulations
description:
socratic-tutor.ts has dialogues for system design but NONE for distributed simulations. Add proactive prompts:
- Raft (after election): 'What if TWO nodes start elections? Try crashing a different node.'
- CAP (after partition): 'What would change in AP mode? Toggle and try.'
- Gossip (after convergence): 'How many rounds did it take? Now kill a node.'
acceptanceCriteria: At least 1 Socratic prompt per simulation
Prompts trigger after specific user actions
Prompts guide discovery, not give answers
User can dismiss/disable tutor
